feedback.text: Hello, thanks to great people like Ross Scott, I have heard about the opportunity for me as a citizen of the EU to participate in making changes in the European Union. Please consider regulating how sold digital goods like computer games are to be handled by the publishers. Consumers/Customers have *bought* a product that would work without (!) remote infrastructure/servers, and yet companies like Ubisoft decided to go for even more money and shut down their game "The Crew" - and this is only one example. A regulator decision on this is fundamental, because it will determine whether customers in the EU actually own what they buy, or whether we better get used to not owning anything, and better be happy about it. Please do something about it, because I am confident you can! You already successfully regulated microtransaction mechanics and lootboxes, and this caused ripple effects for all other consumers world wide. I would say that is real power, but for the good! Thanks for reading this and hopefully taking care of this issue. It is a chance to make other citizens of the EU, that might struggle with believing into the good of the EU (see Brexit) believe again that the EU represents their interests! Kind regards!
